title: ds-viz-timeline - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.timeline. Verifies all options from the Splunk Cloud 10.3 official docs (help.splunk.com/.../visualizations/timelines): category, duration, additionalTooltipFields, backgroundColor, dataColors + dataColorConfig (rangeValue and matchValue), legendDisplay, legendTruncation, resultLimit, seriesColors, yAxisLabelWidth. Demonstrates discrete events (circles) vs intervals (bars), per-event tooltips, dynamic coloring by numeric range and by category match, and editorial layouts.

options.query: | makeresults count=12 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn*2) . "h") | eval category = case(rn%3==0,"deploy", rn%3==1,"alert", 1==1,"audit") | eval label = case(category=="deploy","web-" . tostring(rn) . " deployed", category=="alert","alert: cpu > 90% on host-" . tostring(rn), 1==1,"user_login on host-" . tostring(rn)) | eval severity = case(category=="alert",random()%3+1, 1==1,1) | table _time category label severity

ds_deploys_only.name: deploys only - one lane, simple timeline

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(20-rn*2) . "h") | eval env = case(rn%3==0,"prod", rn%3==1,"staging", 1==1,"dev") | eval label = "deploy v1." . tostring(rn) | table _time env label

ds_attack_types.name: attack types over time - SOC investigation pattern

options.query: | makeresults count=15 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn) . "h") | eval attack_type = case(rn%5==0,"phishing", rn%5==1,"brute_force", rn%5==2,"sql_injection", rn%5==3,"ddos", 1==1,"malware") | eval src_ip = "10.0.0." . tostring(rn) | eval count = rn | table _time attack_type src_ip count

ds_dense.name: dense events - tests visual clutter handling

options.query: | makeresults count=40 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24*60-rn*30) . "m") | eval category = case(rn%4==0,"info", rn%4==1,"warn", rn%4==2,"error", 1==1,"critical") | eval label = category . "_event_" . tostring(rn) | table _time category label

ds_jobs_with_duration.name: batch jobs - intervals (events with duration in seconds)

options.query: | makeresults count=10 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(20-rn*2) . "h") | eval job = case(rn%3==0,"etl-load", rn%3==1,"index-rebuild", 1==1,"backup") | eval duration = case(rn%3==0,1800+rn*60, rn%3==1,3600+rn*120, 1==1,2700+rn*90) | eval status = case(rn%4==0,"failed", 1==1,"success") | table _time job duration status

ds_resource_utilization.name: host CPU sampling - numeric values for dataColorConfig rangeValue

options.query: | makeresults count=18 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn) . "h") | eval host = case(rn%3==0,"db-01", rn%3==1,"web-01", 1==1,"api-01") | eval cpu_pct = case(rn%6==0,12, rn%6==1,28, rn%6==2,45, rn%6==3,62, rn%6==4,78, 1==1,93) | table _time host cpu_pct

ds_changelog_intervals.name: release windows - feature work spanning hours, color by status

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(22-rn*2) . "h") | eval feature = case(rn==1,"auth-rewrite", rn==2,"billing-v2", rn==3,"search-index", rn==4,"alerts-engine", rn==5,"dashboards", rn==6,"data-export", rn==7,"compliance", 1==1,"ml-pipeline") | eval duration = 1800 + (rn * 600) | eval status = case(rn%4==0,"failed", rn%4==1,"in_progress", rn%4==2,"deployed", 1==1,"reverted") | eval owner = case(rn%2==0,"platform-team", 1==1,"product-team") | table _time feature duration status owner

ds_audit_with_user.name: audit events with user + ip - tooltip enrichment example

options.query: | makeresults count=14 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn) . "h") | eval action = case(rn%4==0,"login", rn%4==1,"logout", rn%4==2,"config_change", 1==1,"data_export") | eval user = case(rn%3==0,"alice", rn%3==1,"bob", 1==1,"carol") | eval ip = "192.168.1." . tostring(rn*7) | eval result = case(rn%5==0,"denied", 1==1,"allowed") | table _time action user ip result

ds_pipeline_stages.name: CI pipeline - matchValue color by stage

options.query: | makeresults count=20 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24*60-rn*60) . "m") | eval stage = case(rn%5==0,"build", rn%5==1,"test", rn%5==2,"scan", rn%5==3,"deploy", 1==1,"smoke") | eval duration = case(rn%5==0,180, rn%5==1,420, rn%5==2,90, rn%5==3,300, 1==1,60) | eval branch = case(rn%2==0,"main", 1==1,"feature/x") | table _time stage duration branch

ds_categorical_lanes.name: categorical lanes - 56 events across 7 lanes spanning 4 months, mixed colours per lane (matches Splunk UI doc example)

options.query: | makeresults count=56 | streamstats count AS rn | eval lane = case(rn%7==0,"Lane 1", rn%7==1,"Lane 2", rn%7==2,"Lane 3", rn%7==3,"Lane 4", rn%7==4,"Lane 5", rn%7==5,"Lane 6", 1==1,"Lane 7") | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(120-rn*2) . "d") | eval status = case(rn%5==0,"running", rn%5==1,"degraded", rn%5==2,"resolved", rn%5==3,"investigating", 1==1,"queued") | eval duration = case(rn%4==0,1209600, rn%4==1,604800, rn%4==2,1814400, 1==1,432000) | eval foo = "foo_" . tostring(rn) | eval bar = "bar_" . tostring(rn*3) | table _time lane status duration foo bar

ds_dense_categorical.name: dense categorical lanes - 80 events / 5 lanes, short durations so bars cluster into compound colour strips

options.query: | makeresults count=80 | streamstats count AS rn | eval host = case(rn%5==0,"web-edge", rn%5==1,"api-prod", rn%5==2,"db-primary", rn%5==3,"cache-redis", 1==1,"queue-broker") | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(60-rn*0.7) . "d") | eval severity = case(rn%4==0,"critical", rn%4==1,"high", rn%4==2,"medium", 1==1,"info") | eval duration = case(rn%3==0,86400, rn%3==1,259200, 1==1,172800) | eval owner = case(rn%3==0,"sre-team", rn%3==1,"app-team", 1==1,"data-team") | eval region = case(rn%2==0,"us-east-1", 1==1,"eu-west-1") | table _time host severity duration owner region

ds_user_sessions.name: user sessions - 60 sessions spread across 8 users, action types blended in same lane

options.query: | makeresults count=60 | streamstats count AS rn | eval user = case(rn%8==0,"alice", rn%8==1,"bob", rn%8==2,"carol", rn%8==3,"dave", rn%8==4,"eve", rn%8==5,"frank", rn%8==6,"grace", 1==1,"henry") | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(45-rn*0.7) . "d") | eval action = case(rn%5==0,"login", rn%5==1,"search", rn%5==2,"export", rn%5==3,"config_change", 1==1,"logout") | eval duration = case(rn%5==0,1800, rn%5==1,7200, rn%5==2,3600, rn%5==3,900, 1==1,60) | eval source_ip = "10." . tostring(rn%4) . "." . tostring(rn%256) . "." . tostring(rn%128) | eval result = case(rn%6==0,"denied", 1==1,"allowed") | table _time user action duration source_ip result

options.markdown:
# `splunk.timeline` - option matrix

`splunk.timeline` plots discrete events along a horizontal time axis, optionally grouped into lanes by a `category` field. Events with a `duration` field render as **bars** (intervals); events without one render as **circles**.

Full canonical option surface from [help.splunk.com/...timelines](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-cloud-platform/create-dashboards-and-reports/dashboard-studio/10.3.2512/visualizations/timelines):

- `category` (DOS) - lane field
- `duration` (DOS) - interval length in seconds
- `additionalTooltipFields` - extra fields shown on hover
- `backgroundColor`
- `dataColors` (DOS / array) - dynamic per-event coloring with `dataColorConfig`
- `legendDisplay` (`right` | `bottom` | `off`) and `legendTruncation` (`ellipsisEnd` | `ellipsisMiddle` | `ellipsisStart` | `off`)
- `resultLimit` (default `10000`)
- `seriesColors` - palette array
- `yAxisLabelWidth` (default `100`)

**Use it when**: the story is *when did things happen* and events are sparse and discrete (deploys, alerts, audits, jobs). Use a column or area chart when the story is *how many*.

## Categorical lanes - the canonical Splunk UI Timeline pattern

The three full-width panels below match the canonical *Categorical Timeline* example from the [Splunk UI Timeline package](https://splunkui.splunk.com/Packages/visualizations/Timeline) (the example image with `Lane 1 ... Lane 7`).

The key trick: **many short-duration events per lane, spread across a long time window, coloured by `dataColors` + `matchValue`**. When events crowd together inside a lane, their bars touch and form a compound multi-colour stripe - the visual story stops being *here is one event* and starts being *this lane was busy with a mix of statuses across the period*.

- Panel 1 reproduces the **Categorical Timeline** image: 56 events across 7 lanes spanning 4 months.
- Panel 2 reproduces **Additional Tooltip Fields** with the same dataset (`foo`, `bar` show on hover).
- Panel 3 is a denser variant (80 events / 5 host lanes, severity-coloured) showing how the same pattern reads in a SRE context.

viz_categorical_lanes.title: Categorical Timeline
viz_categorical_lanes.description: Reproduces the canonical Splunk UI example: 7 lanes, ~56 events spread across 4 months, each event coloured by `status` via `matchValue`. The mix of short bars per lane creates the compound multi-colour stripe that defines this layout.

viz_categorical_tooltip.title: Additional Tooltip Fields - ['foo', 'bar']
viz_categorical_tooltip.description: Same data as the panel above, with `additionalTooltipFields: ['foo','bar']`. Hover any bar to see the per-event payload appended to the default tooltip - the canonical pattern for audit timelines where the lane and the time alone are not enough.

viz_categorical_dense.title: Dense categorical - 80 events / 5 host lanes, severity-coloured
viz_categorical_dense.description: Same compound-stripe technique applied to a SRE context: 5 host lanes (web-edge, api-prod, db-primary, cache-redis, queue-broker), 80 incidents over 60 days, coloured by `severity` via `matchValue`. The denser the bars, the easier it is to see *which host had the busiest week, in which colour*.

viz_user_sessions_categorical.title: User session feed - 8 users, mixed action types per lane
viz_user_sessions_categorical.description: Identity / audit pattern. Each lane is a user, each bar is a session, coloured by `action` (login / search / export / config_change / logout). Where one user has many actions in the same week, their lane shows the compound colour stripe - this is what makes timelines so much more readable than a flat event list when the question is *who did what, when, and in what order*.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - no options, single lane
viz_default.description: Bare minimum. Events have only a start time, so they render as circles. Without `category` the viz infers a single default lane.

viz_category.title: 2. category - one lane per value
viz_category.description: Set `category` (DOS) to the field name and each unique value gets its own horizontal lane. This is the workhorse pattern.

viz_duration_intervals.title: 3. duration - intervals as bars
viz_duration_intervals.description: Add a numeric `duration` field (seconds) and events become horizontal bars whose length encodes how long they took. Mixed with discrete events, the chart switches per-row between circle and bar. Set `duration` to `0` if you want a circle for that specific event.

viz_release_windows.title: 4. Real intervals - release windows by feature
viz_release_windows.description: Eight feature releases. `duration` makes each one a horizontal bar; lanes group by `feature`. Read horizontally for *how long did this take*; read vertically for *what was happening at this moment*.

viz_data_color_ranges.title: 5. dataColors + rangeValue - CPU heatmap
viz_data_color_ranges.description: Color events dynamically by numeric value. `dataColors` is bound via DOS to `cpu_pct` and resolved through `dataColorConfig` ranges: cool blue at 0-30%, amber at 30-60%, red at 60-100%. This is the canonical SOC / NOC pattern: severity-tinted events read at a glance.

viz_data_color_match.title: 6. dataColors + matchValue - status semantics
viz_data_color_match.description: Color by exact match against a string field. Each `status` value gets its own color via `matchValue(dataColorConfig)`. Use this for traffic-light semantics where red/amber/green is non-negotiable.

viz_match_pipeline.title: 7. matchValue - CI pipeline stages
viz_match_pipeline.description: Twenty CI runs colored by stage. `matchValue` makes the same stage (build, test, scan, deploy, smoke) keep the same color across the entire timeline so the eye learns the rhythm.

viz_tooltip_fields.title: 8. additionalTooltipFields - enriched hover
viz_tooltip_fields.description: Hover over each event to see `user`, `ip`, and `result` appended to the default tooltip. Indispensable for audit trails where the timestamp alone isn't enough context.

viz_legend_off.title: 9. legendDisplay: off
viz_legend_off.description: Hide the legend entirely. The default for `splunk.timeline` is actually `off`; this panel is here so the matrix is complete.

viz_legend_bottom.title: 10. legendDisplay: bottom + legendTruncation
viz_legend_bottom.description: Move the legend below. `legendTruncation` controls how long category names are shortened when they overflow: `ellipsisEnd` (default), `ellipsisMiddle`, `ellipsisStart`, or `off`.

viz_yaxis_wide.title: 11. yAxisLabelWidth - wider lane labels
viz_yaxis_wide.description: Default is 100px. Bump to 200 when category names are long (feature flags, customer IDs). Otherwise labels truncate and lose meaning. Pair with `legendDisplay: off` so the lanes themselves carry the labeling.

viz_result_limit_low.title: 12. resultLimit: 6 - performance throttle
viz_result_limit_low.description: Default is 10000. Lower it when you have very dense events and only the first N matter (e.g. preview panels, embedded summaries). Truncation is hard - the chart shows only the first 6 rows, in source order, no top-N selection.

viz_custom_colors.title: 13. seriesColors - custom palette
viz_custom_colors.description: Override the auto-generated palette with `seriesColors` (must be an array - same schema rule as Sankey: CSV strings are rejected). Order maps to series order (alphabetical on the category field).

viz_bg_color.title: 14. backgroundColor - editorial tint
viz_bg_color.description: Tint the events area. Pairs well with bold series colors for SOC walls and night-mode dashboards.

viz_attack_types_full.title: 15. SOC pattern - attack types (canonical example from docs)
viz_attack_types_full.description: Mirrors the canonical example from the official Splunk docs: attack categories on lanes, full Splunk Prisma palette via seriesColors, legend on the right. This is the layout most production SOC dashboards converge on.

viz_deploys.title: 16. Deploy timeline - env lanes
viz_deploys.description: Deploys grouped by environment. Three lanes (prod, staging, dev), three colors. Simple, scannable.

viz_dense.title: 17. Dense events - 40 events stress test
viz_dense.description: Stress test with 40 events across 4 categories. Demonstrates how the viz handles overlapping markers - they cluster but remain interactive on hover.

viz_no_title.description: No `title` - the timeline becomes a section element rather than a labeled panel. Pair with a markdown header for editorial layouts where the heading is part of the page composition rather than the panel chrome.
